components.name: Footing
components.purpose: Spreads structural loads from columns/walls into the soil below safe bearing capacity.
components.function: Distributes point and line loads over a wider area to prevent bearing failure.
components.resilienceBenefit: Proper footing prevents differential settlement during floods and earthquakes.

components.name: Foundation
components.purpose: Transfers entire building load to ground; includes footings, raft, or piles.
components.function: Anchors structure against uplift and lateral loads.
components.engineeringPrinciple: Load path continuity from superstructure to bearing stratum.
components.resilienceBenefit: Flood-resilient foundations use piles or deep footings above scour depth.

components.name: Plinth
components.purpose: Raises living floor above ground moisture and flood level.
components.function: Damp-proof course and level transfer between foundation and walls.
components.engineeringPrinciple: Hydrostatic pressure blocked by DPC and height above BFE.
components.resilienceBenefit: Primary flood defense in raised plinth designs.

components.name: Column
components.purpose: Vertical load-bearing member transferring loads from beams/slabs to foundation.
components.function: Carries axial compression and resists lateral seismic/wind forces.
components.engineeringPrinciple: Euler buckling and moment capacity per IS/BS codes.
failureModes: Lap splice in plastic hinge zone
constructionTips: Stagger splices away from beam-column joints
components.resilienceBenefit: Ductile columns with ties improve earthquake survival.

components.name: Beam
components.purpose: Horizontal member spanning openings and supporting slabs/roof.
components.function: Resists bending and shear; ties structure together as frame.
constructionTips: Cast beam-column joints monolithically where possible
components.resilienceBenefit: Continuous beam bands improve seismic load path.

components.name: Lintel Band
components.purpose: Horizontal reinforcement band at opening heads tying walls together.
components.engineeringPrinciple: Confined masonry requires continuous horizontal bands at sill, lintel, and roof levels.
components.resilienceBenefit: Critical for earthquake resistance in masonry houses.

components.name: Wall
components.purpose: Vertical enclosure and load-bearing or shear element.
components.function: Resists gravity and in-plane lateral loads.
components.engineeringPrinciple: Slenderness ratio and aspect ratio govern failure mode.
components.resilienceBenefit: Confined panels improve ductility under seismic load.

components.name: Roof Joint
components.purpose: Connects roof diaphragm to walls for lateral load transfer.
components.function: Transfers wind/seismic inertia to shear walls.
components.engineeringPrinciple: Diaphragm action requires positive connection at eaves.
constructionTips: Use hurricane ties in wind zones
Keep roof light in seismic zones
components.resilienceBenefit: Light roof reduces seismic demand on structure.
